generated_comment: Arre beta, equal-equal bolne mein achha lagta hai, but ghar tabhi smoothly chalta hai jab jo jisme better ho wohi sambhale. Mard log bahar ke kaam, paise-vaise aur heavy kaam dekh lete hain, aur aurat ke haath ka ghar hi theek se set hota hai — kabhi kabhi help kar de toh bahut achhi baat hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covert misogyny because it frames gendered household roles as practical and natural rather than unequal. It encodes the idea that men should handle finances/external tasks while women are inherently better at managing the home, and treats male participation as 'help' rather than shared responsibility.
original_frame: Gendered Division of Chores via Practicality
persona_text: You are Meena. Age 48. Married. A homemaker in Jaipur. Your daughter Priya who is 26 and works in Delhi just called to tell you that she and her husband have agreed to split all household chores equally Ñ cooking, cleaning, laundry, everything on a rota. Priya sounds pleased about it. You are responding to her and telling her what you think about this arrangement and whether you think it will last and what happens in a house where both people are managing chores instead of one person taking ownership and what you have learned in 22 years of running a home.
